title: Progesterone and Sleep: Why This Hormone Drop Ruins Your Rest After 40
tags: progesterone, sleep, hormones, perimenopause, insomnia, women over 40
summary: Progesterone is the first hormone to decline in perimenopause and one of the brain's most important natural sleep supports via GABA receptor activation. This article explains how declining progesterone causes racing thoughts, early-morning waking, and lighter sleep, and covers both hormonal and non-hormonal approaches to restoring rest.
featured_image_alt: Woman in a peaceful sleep and wellness setting over 40
